details.response:
The annual leave entitlement for employees is as follows:
- Junior level employees with fewer than 3 years of service are entitled to 5 working days per year.
- Mid-level employees with 3 to 10 years of service are entitled to 10 working days per year.
- Senior-level employees with more than 10 years of service are entitled to 15 working days per year [3].

Regarding the carryover policy, unused annual leave may be carried over into the next calendar year, but it is subject to the following rules:
- Each employee can carry over a maximum of 5 days of annual leave.
- The carried-over leave must be used within the next calendar year [1].

This policy applies to all full-time employees and does not apply to vendor onboarding workflows or customer ticket routing procedures [2].
retrieved_contexts:
Document: Annual Leave Carryover
Section Path: Annual Leave Carryover > Policy Rules
Department: HR

Content:
## Policy Rules
Under company policy, unused annual leave may be carried over into the next year, subject to the following rules:
- Each employee may carry over a maximum of 5 days of annual leave.
- Carried-over leave must be used within the next calendar year.

Document: Annual Leave Carryover
Section Path: Annual Leave Carryover > Scope
Department: HR

Content:
## Scope
This policy applies to all full-time employees. It does not apply to vendor onboarding workflows or customer ticket routing procedures.

Document: Employee Handbook v3.2
Section Path: Employee Handbook v3.2 > Chapter 3: Leave and Benefits > 3.1 Annual Leave
Department: HR

Content:
### 3.1 Annual Leave
- Junior level with fewer than 3 years of service: 5 working days per year
- Mid level with 3 to 10 years of service: 10 working days per year
- Senior level with more than 10 years of service: 15 working days per year

Unused annual leave may be carried over to the next calendar year, up to a maximum of 5 days.
